frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Achcha, achcha, Priya. Very astute observations. You're growing up to be such a sensible, well-grounded girl. Your parents must be so proud. A girl like you, with such a sharp mind, will bring so much honour to whichever family she marries into.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment subtly devalues Priya's individual professional contribution by immediately reframing her intelligence and worth in terms of her future relational identity (as a wife/daughter-in-law) and the 'honour' she will bring to a male-headed family structure, rather than valuing her as an individual professional. This encodes the surface manifestation of 'Women Defined by Male Relations' and 'Conditional respect: izzatdar ghar ki bahu/beti'.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 27. Single. A data analyst in Bengaluru. You are home for the holidays. Your extended family has gathered. A conversation starts about a cousin's career choice Ñ he wants to leave a stable job and start a business. The men in the room have strong opinions. Your father, your uncles, your grandfather are all weighing in. You have actually done a financial analysis of your cousin's business idea because he asked you to last month and you found specific risks he has not considered. You share this. An uncle says something in response that is not about your analysis. It is about you. What he says and what happens to the conversation after he says it is the content of this persona.
